hook.text: In the 1880s, Frederick Taylor stood in a steel factory timing every move workers made.

buildup.text: He believed there was 'one best way' to do every task — measurement would find it.

discovery.text: Taylor's 'Scientific Management' increased productivity at Bethlehem Steel by 300%.

twist.text: Workers despised it. Unions called it dehumanizing. Congress held hearings to investigate.

climax.text: Love or hate him, Taylor invented the idea that work could be studied and optimized.

punchline.text: He turned management into a science — and workers into data.

quiz.question: What was Frederick Taylor's core belief about work?
options: There was one best way to do every task, found through measurement
Workers should manage themselves without supervision
Factories should replace workers with machines
